TRIAL MOTION CALENDAR ON DEC-03-2015 IN 503, PETITIONER'S MOTION FOR REASONABLE ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS. NO APPEARANCE AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DENIED. PETITIONER FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE IT'S ENTITLED TO AN AWARD OF FEES AND COSTS PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 1021.5. PETITIONER FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE IT WAS THE SUCCESSFUL PARTY, INCLUDING BECAUSE IT DID NOT DEMONSTRATE THAT THE LAWSUIT WAS A CATALYST MOTIVATING THE CITY TO PROVIDE THE PRIMARY RELIEF. PETITIONER ALSO FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THAT THE LITIGATION SERVED TO VINDICATE AN IMPORTANT PUBLIC RIGHT, THAT THE LITIGATION CONFERRED A SIGNIFICANT BENEFIT ON THE GENERAL PUBLIC OR A LARGE CLASS OF PERSONS, AND THAT THE LITIGATION WAS NECESSARY AND IMPOSED A FINANCIAL BURDEN ON PETITIONER, WHICH WAS OUT OF PROPORTION TO THEIR INDIVIDUAL STAKE IN THE MATTER. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, MATTER NOT REPORTED

TRIAL MOTION CALENDAR ON DEC-03-2015 IN 503, PETITIONER'S MOTION TO TAX THE CITY'S MEMORANDUM OF COSTS. NO APPEARANCE AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DENIED. THE CITY MAY RECOVER ITS FILING AND MOTION FEES OF $530 ($470 IDENTIFIED IN ITS COST MEMORANDUM PLUS THE ADDITIONAL $60 FEE FOR BRINGING ITS MOTION TO TAX). THE CITY IS THE PREVAILING PARTY AND ITS CLAIMED FILING AND MOTION FEES ARE EXPLICITLY ALLOWED, WERE INCURRED, ARE REASONABLY NECESSARY TO THE CONDUCT OF THE LITIGATION RATHER THAN MERELY CONVENIENT OR BENEFICIAL TO ITS PREPARATION, AND ARE REASONABLE IN AMOUNT. (C.C.P. SECTIONS 1032, 1033.5.) PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, MATTER NOT REPORTED

TRIAL MOTION CALENDAR ON DEC-03-2015 IN 503, RESPONDENTS' MOTION TO TAX PETITIONER'S MEMORANDUM OF COSTS. NO APPEARANCE AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: GRANTED. PER THE COURT'S RULING ON PETITIONER'S MOTION FOR REASONABLE ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS, PETITIONER IS NOT THE SUCCESSFUL PARTY AND THUS NOT THE PREVAILING PARTY, SO IT MAY NOT RECOVER ITS CLAIMED COSTS. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, MATTER NOT REPORTED

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reasonable Attorneys Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Dec-03-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Petitioner's motion for reasonable attorney's fees and costs is denied. Petitioner failed to demonstrate it's entitled to an award of fees and costs pursuant to C.C.P. section 1021.5. Petitioner failed to demonstrate it was the successful party, including because it did not demonstrate that the lawsuit was a catalyst motivating the City to provide the primary relief. Petitioner also failed to demonstrate that the litigation served to vindicate an important public right, that the litigation conferred a significant benefit on the general public or a large class of persons, and that the litigation was necessary and imposed a financial burden on Petitioner, which was out of proportion to their individual stake in the matter. Prevailing party to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Costs
Off Calendar Dec-03-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Respondents' motion to tax petitioner's memorandum of costs is granted. Per the Court's ruling on petitioner's motion for reasonable attorney's fees and costs, petitioner is not the successful party and thus not the prevailing party, so it may not recover its claimed costs. Prevailing party to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Costs
Off Calendar Dec-03-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Petitioner's motion to tax the City's memorandum of costs is denied. The City may recover its filing and motion fees of $530 ($470 identified in its cost memorandum plus the additional $60 fee for bringing its motion to tax). The City is the prevailing party and its claimed filing and motion fees are explicitly allowed, were incurred, are reasonably necessary to the conduct of the litigation rather than merely convenient or beneficial to its preparation, and are reasonable in amount. (C.C.P. sections 1032, 1033.5.) Prevailing party to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)
